reason: Karpathy loop script invokes kiro-cli sub-agents via bash subprocess for blind A/B/C evaluation. This EOD run was triggered as a direct agent turn from user invocation rather than the scheduled hook. To avoid spawning a nested kiro-cli process that could run minutes-to-hours unattended during a direct session, Phase 6 is deferred to the next automated EOD hook run (Monday). Eligible priors data intact in autoresearch_priors — no loss.

suggestions.description: Adi Thakur asked for Kiro setup help in next 1:1 DM today. Concrete adoption signal. Draft a short 'Kiro for Paid Acq' onboarding doc (30-min install + 3 starter prompts) and share in next 1:1 — this is the L3 graduation metric ('one tool adopted by teammate'). Reversible.
suggestions.measurable: Adi completes setup, runs 1 agent command successfully in his own environment.

suggestions.description: Hard thing has been at zero for 20 workdays. Testing Approach v5 for Brandon. Subtraction experiment: block 90 minutes Monday 9-10:30am before any Slack/email — ship a rough v5 draft (worse = shipped, not worse = not shipped). Protect the habit loop by moving Monday Today slate to 2 items only: Testing Approach v5 draft + AU account check.
suggestions.measurable: Monday EOD: does v5 draft exist, regardless of quality?

suggestions.description: Wiki pipeline emitted 24 DRAFTs + 7 merges today but 0 promoted to FINAL. Test whether a 30-min Monday morning critic-review pass produces 3+ FINAL articles. If yes, promote the pattern to a Friday recurring task. If no, pipeline bottleneck is human review, not drafting — different intervention needed.
suggestions.measurable: Monday 9:30am: critic reviews 5 DRAFTs. Count FINALs by EOD.
